data.title: ABM reaffirms $3.65–$3.80 EPS guidance as record $1.1B bookings and segment growth drive outlook
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: ABM Industries Incorporated (ABM) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Scott B. Salmirs, President and CEO, highlighted a return to organic growth in both Business & Industry (B&I) and Manufacturing & Distribution (M&D), improved cash flow, and record $1.1 billion in new bookings during the first half. "Overall, we posted 3.8% organic revenue growth, highlighted by continued recovery in our core commercial office markets, new contract wins and a diminished impact from prior year of client exits in M&D." He reported strong performances in Aviation and Education, noting temporary project delays in ATS but emphasizing market health and expectations for a strong year.
  • Salmirs stated, "We expect delayed projects from Q2 to resume in the third quarter and are reaffirming our full year adjusted EPS guidance."
  • He described segment highlights, including B&I's positive momentum due to improving prime office indicators, M&D's earlier-than-expected return to organic growth, new $190 million microgrid business, large Aviation contract wins, and $700 million Technical Solutions backlog. He added, "We believe our capabilities and our strategies to enhance them are fully in line with where demand is going."
  • Earl Ray Ellis, Executive VP & CFO, addressed a definitional update to non-GAAP financial measures after SEC communication, noting, "Importantly, there was no impact to our current quarter's results."
  • Ellis reported, "Revenue grew 4.6% year-over-year to $2.1 billion, driven by 3.8% organic growth and contributions from our 2024 acquisition of Quality Uptime Services."
  • Ellis added, "Free cash flow for the quarter was $15 million, an improvement of $138 million over quarter 1."

Outlook

  • ABM reaffirmed its full year adjusted EPS guidance of $3.65 to $3.80 and an adjusted EBITDA margin between 6.3% and 6.5%. Management expects normalized free cash flow for the full year in the range of $250 million to $290 million, excluding $30 million to $40 million of ELEVATE and integration costs and any portion of the RavenVolt earn-out payment.
  • Ellis stated, "We are maintaining our interest expense forecast of $80 million to $84 million and continue to expect a normalized tax rate before discrete items of 29% to 30%."
  • Management expects delayed Q2 projects to resume in Q3, supporting the outlook for both revenue and margin improvement in the second half.

Financial Results

  • ABM reported $2.1 billion in revenue for the quarter and adjusted EPS of $0.86. Net income was $42.2 million or $0.67 per diluted share. Adjusted net income was $54.1 million or $0.86 per diluted share.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $125.9 million and adjusted EBITDA margin was 6.2%.
  • B&I revenue reached $1 billion, with a 3% increase and a 40 basis point margin improvement. Aviation revenue was $260.1 million, up 9%, with operating profit up 26%. M&D generated $398.1 million in revenue, a 2% increase, while Education saw 1% revenue growth. Technical Solutions delivered 19% revenue growth to $210.2 million, with margin pressure from project mix and timing.
  • Available liquidity was $657.8 million, including $58.7 million in cash and cash equivalents. Total indebtedness was $1.6 billion.

Q&A

  • Timothy Michael Mulrooney, William Blair: Asked about the RavenVolt earn-out, cash flow bridge to the full year target, and B&I organic growth trajectory. Ellis explained the earn-out expectations and cash flow normalization, stating, "We believe that we're going to have incremental improvements in cash flow for both Q3 and Q4." Salmirs said, "We like to believe now we're in positive organic growth territory for B&I from here on out."
  • Andrew John Wittmann, Baird: Inquired about M&D's expanded service offerings and margin dynamics. Salmirs described the strategic move to higher-margin, stickier services in core client operations. Regarding margin, he noted investments in sales talent and strategic pricing rather than broad-based market competitiveness.
  • Faiza Alwy, Deutsche Bank: Asked about project delays in Technical Solutions and Education segment trends. Ellis clarified that project delays were timing-related and margins are expected to normalize. Salmirs described Education as "pretty normalized" with renewals and investment trends supporting a stable outlook.
  • Jasper James Bibb, Truist Securities: Queried about organic growth trajectory and details on battery energy storage contract. Salmirs highlighted a record $700 million backlog and ongoing client demand despite tariff and tax credit uncertainties.
  • Joshua K. Chan, UBS: Sought detail on ATS margin impacts from project delays and mix. Salmirs broke down the margin effect as related to project phase timing. Chan also asked about B&I market positioning, with Salmirs emphasizing ABM's scale, execution, and market share in prime office spaces.
  • Marc Frye Riddick, Sidoti: Asked about cash usage prioritization and M&A pipeline. Ellis described an active M&A environment with focus on deals that enhance ABM's strategic positioning.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on cash flow normalization, margin sustainability, and growth trajectory, expressing generally positive sentiment with detailed follow-ups on execution challenges and segment strategy.
  • Management maintained a confident tone throughout, frequently reaffirming guidance and emphasizing operational progress and market opportunities. Salmirs stated, "We're confident this investment will enhance our service delivery, improve operational efficiency and further elevate the client experience."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts displayed increased optimism, with less focus on execution risk related to ERP implementation and more emphasis on segment-specific growth.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language remained steady, with management reaffirming the $3.65 to $3.80 adjusted EPS range and free cash flow target. The major shift was the earlier-than-expected return to organic growth in both B&I and M&D, compared to expectations in the previous quarter for growth to resume later in the year.
  • There was increased discussion of segment expansion, especially in M&D and Technical Solutions, and a stronger tone regarding market recovery, particularly in prime office and Aviation.
  • Analysts' questions evolved from ERP and backlog concerns to more granular inquiries about segment-specific drivers and pipeline sustainability.
  • Management's confidence level increased, supported by record bookings and a positive cash flow trajectory.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited potential risks from project approval delays, working capital normalization, and possible changes to tax credits for energy projects, but indicated these are being managed with operational adjustments and ongoing client engagement.
  • Analysts asked about margin pressures in M&D and Technical Solutions, with management attributing these to strategic investments and project timing rather than systemic competitive threats.
  • The ERP implementation continues to be monitored, though progress has reduced operational friction.

Final Takeaway

Management emphasized that ABM's return to organic growth in key segments, record $1.1 billion in new bookings, and reaffirmed guidance for both EPS and free cash flow position the company for continued margin improvement and expansion. The focus remains on leveraging scale, integrated technology, and strategic investments to capture growth opportunities in core markets, while ongoing operational enhancements and a robust M&A pipeline support long-term shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Westbridge Renewable launches normal course issuer bid, to buyback up to 5% of shares
data.description:
• Westbridge Renewable Energy (WEB: CA) (OTCQX:WEGYF) received TSXV approval to initiate a new normal course issuer bid (NCIB) starting June 9, 2025.
  • The program allows for the repurchase of up to 5.06M common shares, or ~5% of total outstanding shares.
  • All repurchased shares will be cancelled.
  • The NCIB will end on June 8, 2026, or earlier if the full amount is bought back.
  • The company plans to fund the NCIB with available cash.
  • In a prior NCIB (March 2024–March 2025), Westbridge repurchased and cancelled 693,800 shares.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: JBDI regains Nasdaq bid price compliance, addresses financial filing deficiency
data.description:
• JBDI Holdings (NASDAQ:JBDI) received Nasdaq confirmation on June 3, 2025, that it regained compliance with the $1 minimum bid price requirement.
  • On the same day, Nasdaq issued a deficiency notice due to JBDI’s delayed filing of its quarterly financials.
  • JBDI filed the required financials on June 5, 2025, and plans to inform Nasdaq to resolve the compliance issue.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Greg Kidd to acquire controlling interest in Know Labs
data.description:
• Know Labs (NYSE:KNW) announced on Friday that it has entered into an agreement with Goldeneye 1995 - an affiliate of fintech investor Greg Kidd - to acquire a controlling interest in the company.
  • Upon the closing of the transaction, Greg Kidd will become chief executive officer and chairman of the board of directors of the company.
  • Under the terms of the agreement, Goldeneye 1995 will acquire that number of shares of the company’s common stock obtained by dividing (i) the sum of 1,000 Bitcoin and a cash sum to retire existing debt, redeem outstanding preferred equity and provide additional working capital by the per-share purchase price of $0.335.
  • The Bitcoin will be employed by the company’s treasury strategy, providing investors with significant economic exposure to this highly attractive digital asset, said the company.
  • The company said, "When the Bitcoin holdings become the principal component of the company’s balance sheet, management will use the multiple of net asset value metric to measure the premium investors ascribe to the company’s market valuation relative to its Bitcoin holdings."
  • Founder Ron Erickson will serve as President of a new division that will retain a team of scientists to continue the company’s proprietary diagnostic research, said the company, adding that, "Upon the closing of the transaction, Erickson will become vice chairman of the Board."
  • The transaction, which was unanimously approved by the Know Labs board of directors, is expected to close in the third quarter of 2025.
  • KNW is +80.39% to $0.92.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Diana Shipping charters Panamax vessel, secures $3.6M in revenue
data.description:
• Diana Shipping (NYSE:DSX) signed a time charter with Stone Shipping for its Ice Class Panamax vessel Atalandi.
  • Charter starts June 9, 2025, at $9,000/day for the first 35 days, then $10,100/day, minus 5% commission, through at least June 15, 2026 (max August 15, 2026).
  • The charter is expected to generate ~$3.62M in gross revenue for the minimum term.
  • Diana’s fleet now includes 37 dry bulk vessels totaling about 4.1M dwt, with an average age of 11.6 years.
  • Two new methanol dual-fuel Kamsarmax vessels are expected by late 2027 and early 2028.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Recursion rises on launch of Boltz-2 in partnership with MIT
data.description:
• Recursion (NASDAQ:RXRX) shares rose after the company and MIT launched Boltz-2, an open-source biomolecular model.
  • Boltz-2 integrates structure and binding affinity predictions and was developed using the NVIDIA-powered supercomputer, BioHive-2. (NVDA)
  • The stock of the company is up 10% in morning trade.

data.title: S&P pops over 6,000, Nasdaq and Dow also rally as labor report beats consensus
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages jumped on Friday, and yields rose, as the labor report showed slower growth in May but still reported a healthy gain.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) is +1% as the index marched back above 6,000 for the first time since February 26.

Also climbing is the tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) which is +1.1%, and the Dow (DJI) which is +1.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 7 basis points to 4.46%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 9 basis points to 4.00%.

May Nonfarm payrolls: +139K vs. +126K consensus and +147K prior (revised from +177K), according to data released by the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics on Friday.

Unemployment rate: 4.2% vs. 4.2% consensus and 4.2% prior. 

The indication of a potential thaw in tensions between Musk and Trump comes after a public feud that erupted between them on Thursday. Earlier, their clash escalated when the president threatened to terminate Musk’s government contracts, leading to a 14% drop in Tesla’s (TSLA) shares.

Wall Street closed in the red as Tesla (TSLA) shaved off more than $100B in market capitalization amid Trump and Musk's verbal spat. 

"Markets had a volatile session yesterday, as they grappled with a barrage of news that each pushed in different directions. Those included positive U.S.-China headlines amid a call between Trump and Xi, a hawkish ECB decision, and more weak data from the U.S. But the most remarkable was an extraordinary war of words between Trump and Elon Musk that ultimately left risk assets losing ground," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

The trade headlines outweighed an initial negative reaction to the latest weekly U.S. jobless claims, which added to fears that the U.S. labor market was finally deteriorating after Liberation Day, Reid added. 

Trump on Thursday said that he had held a "very good phone call" with China's leader Xi Jinping which "resulted in a very positive conclusion" for the two nations.

data.title: Nanalysis announces note offering of $2 million
data.description:
• Nanalysis Scientific (TSXV:NSCI:CA) announced on Friday an offering of 12% unsecured promissory notes for aggregate gross proceeds of $2 million.
  • The notes will bear simple interest at a rate of 12% per annum, with interest payable annually within 30 days of the first and second anniversaries of the issuance date of the notes.
  •  At the Company's option, the interest may be paid in cash or common shares of the company. The notes will mature two years from the date of issuance.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for general corporate purposes and to support ongoing business operations.
  • The company said, "Pathfinder Asset Management has agreed to subscribe for $1,000,000 principal amount of Notes under the Offering. In the event that other investors subscribe for less than the remaining $1,000,000 amount of the offering, Pathfinder will increase its subscription amount to a maximum of $2,000,000."
  • In connection with the notes, the company will issue its common shares of the to the Lenders. The bonus shares will represent a value equal to 20% of the principal amount of the notes, calculated based on the Market Price of $0.25 per bonus share. An aggregate of 1.6 million bonus shares will be issued to the lenders. The Bonus Shares will be subject to a four-month hold period commencing from the date of issuance.
  • The company said it may pay a finder's fee of up to 5% of the gross proceeds of the Notes, payable in cash.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: B. Riley granted exception by Nasdaq after additional delinquency notice
data.description:
B. Riley Financial (NASDAQ:RILY) stock was rising as the financial services company said it has been granted an exception from Nasdaq in connection with the failure to timely file financial reports with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

Shares were +4.44% on Friday during morning trade to $3.06.

B. Riley received an additional delinquency notice from Nasdaq on June 4.

The letter indicated the company was not in compliance with the stock exchange's listing rules as a result of the delayed filing of the annual report on Form 10-K for the period ended December 31, 2024, and the quarterly report on Form 10-Q for the period ended March 30, 2025.

Nasdaq's staff has determined to grant an exception to enable the company to regain compliance with the rule after further review and the materials submitted on June 2. The exception stands until  September 29, 2025.

The company said it regrets the delays resulting from a confluence of significant events and transactions completed in 2024. RILY said it is is working to file the reports as promptly as practical, and expects to return to a normal filing cadence by Fall 2025.

data.title: Fmr. St. Louis Fed Pres. Bullard: The Fed will hold until September
data.description:
Former St. Louis Fed President James Bullard believes the latest employment report showing a gain of 139,000 jobs reinforces expectations that the Federal Reserve will keep interest rates on hold until September.

In an interview with CNBC, Bullard suggested the Fed could then potentially lower the policy rate if inflation continues to decline. The positive economic indicators point toward stability in the near term, according to the former Fed official.

“If inflation continues to come down, which is what happened in the last trade war, then the Fed would be in a good position to probably lower the policy rate a little bit in the fall.” He highlighted that current conditions are favorable from the Fed’s perspective, citing low unemployment and inflation that appear to be moderating.

Regarding global economic concerns around tariffs, Bullard acknowledged that the previous trade war with China did slow the global economy. However, he expressed optimism that the proposed 10% across-the-board tariff “looks to me like it’s not going to pass legal muster,” which he characterized as “probably good news” from a macroeconomic perspective. He added that more targeted, temporary tariffs would likely be a better approach.

When questioned about fiscal deficits, Bullard downplayed immediate concerns about recent legislation.

“It looks horrible from a deficit perspective, but I would interpret this bill as being more or less business as usual,” he remarked. “The news about fiscal deficits being bad is not really news. That’s something we’ve known for a long time.”

Bullard drew parallels to 2019, noting that if inflation continues to decline as it did then, the Fed could be positioned to make modest rate cuts later this year. “By the time you got to the summer of 2019, that committee was able to lower the policy rate a little bit, and that put the U.S. economy in pretty good shape,” he explained, “but other things can happen, and why not wait and see?”

More on the U.S. Economy:

  • Nonfarm payrolls growth slows in May, still it's a healthy gain
  • Fed's Schmid says tariffs will start feeding into prices in coming months
  • U.S. trade deficit narrows sharply in April as imports plunge
  • Weak Jobs And Rising Inflation Fuel Stagflation Fears
